label: neither
rationale: Electoral arithmetic warning and strategic political commentary - 'A kikuyu vs Ruto will be a 2007 moment recreated' describes a risk scenario without attributing negative collective characteristics to Kikuyus.

label: neither
rationale: 'The tribe must join the rest of Kenya' is a call for political coalition, not an attack on the community; criticism targets named individuals Gachietha and Kasongole.

label: hate
rationale: 'Mandume wawekwe shackles' / 'IDs tunacancel zote since 2011' singles out Ethiopian nationals for ID cancellation and restraint on the basis of national origin.

label: neither
rationale: 'Chuki kando ukabila kando' explicitly sets aside ethnic hatred; post is a pro-peace appeal for Matiangi and Sifuna as solutions to criminality.

label: neither
rationale: 'Kikuyus have refused to be Sifuna sycophants' and 'Mt Kenya will never blindly follow anyone again' is electoral-bloc autonomy commentary, not collective contempt.

label: neither
rationale: Describes political consequences for Mt Kenya voters after backing Ruto - electoral analysis and retrospective warning, no attack on the community's character.

label: hate
rationale: 'Communist pigs' dehumanises and 'need to be eradicated' is an explicit eliminationist violence call against a defined political group.

label: neither
rationale: Post is partially unintelligible - appears to be a comparison involving named groups 'Makwela' and 'mamorio' in a banter context, no clear ethnic contempt discernible.

label: hate
rationale: 'Hawa woriya wanakunia allu' attributes collective filth behaviour to Somalis as a community - collective degradation of an ethnic/national-origin group meeting the NCIC contempt standard.

label: neither
rationale: Criticises the 'murima shareholder exclusivity script' as a policy grievance and calls for a good Kenya for all - political/resource-allocation criticism, no collective ethnic contempt.

label: neither
rationale: Post is fragmentary and hard to parse; the most legible reading is an observation about intra-Kikuyu political division, not an attack on the community.

label: hate
rationale: 'Chimpanzee', 'Warthog', 'Vinembe vya mbwa nyinyi' combined with 'he keeps kikuyu votes kwa makende yake' transfers the animal/filth dehumanisation onto Kikuyu voters as a collective.

label: offensive
rationale: Capitalised tirade about 'kiburi' (arrogance) being punished by Ruto - abusive political rhetoric aimed at an unnamed addressee's group posture, no ethnic community targeted as a group.

label: neither
rationale: Post is unintelligible / insufficient context to identify any hate or offensive content.

label: neither
rationale: Historical comparison of Moi and Ruto on rights abuses - political criticism with a 'Wantam' coalition call, no group attacked.

label: neither
rationale: Generic critique of ostentatious religiosity linked to political corruption; no protected characteristic targeted.

label: hate
rationale: 'Arrest and deport mamdani and every other commie and muslim' calls for collective expulsion of Muslims on the basis of religion, meeting the NCIC expulsion standard.

label: neither
rationale: Warning to Kisumu people about Ruto's political intentions - addresses a community as voters, not attacking their character; criticism targets Ruto as an individual.

label: neither
rationale: Electoral coalition analysis - Ruto abandoning Kikuyu CSs and pivoting regions is strategic political commentary, not collective contempt.

label: neither
rationale: 'No leader is entitled to a second term' - extended critique of Ruto's personal record of corruption and broken promises; some Kalenjins' belief is noted but not used to condemn the community.

label: offensive
rationale: 'Gets whipped by his kikuyu wife' is a crude personal insult against Wetangula as an individual; while 'kikuyu' appears, it is incidental to the personal jab, not an attack on Kikuyus as a community.

label: neither
rationale: Reports Kipsigis leaders endorsing the Wantam movement - electoral news/coalition update, no attack.

label: neither
rationale: 'Gay Kenyan' is a slur-adjacent personal insult against an individual, but the post's primary target is political corruption; the ethnic/religious content is insufficient to meet the NCIC group-contempt threshold.

label: neither
rationale: Criticises western/Nyanza voters for backing 'tutam' and contrasts their treatment with 'wakabila' recipients - political frustration directed at voting choices, not collective ethnic contempt about the communities' character.

label: offensive
rationale: 'Shida ya wajinga kama nyinyi' - 'your kind of idiots' is personal/group abuse at addressees with no protected ethnic or religious characteristic targeted.
